title: SQL Repositories, Pools, Transactions, and Request Consistency · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective context-bound repository and transaction contract; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in context-bound repository and transaction contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Treat sql.DB as a concurrent pool, open once, verify readiness, tune open and idle capacity and lifetime, observe Stats, and close at process shutdown.
Use dialect parameters, QueryContext, Scan, rows Close and Err, bounded result sets, and error identity without constructing SQL from input.
Define atomic invariants, BeginTx with context and isolation, defer rollback, commit exactly once, and handle PostgreSQL Read Committed changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Inspect the failure record · Debug Db Handle Pool Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 1514 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify transaction outcome without retrying an ambiguous commit; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat sql.DB as a concurrent pool, open once, verify readiness, tune open and idle capacity and lifetime, observe Stats, and close at process shutdown.
